review_reason: Phase 6 blocked on CanonicalR irreflexivity: NoMaxOrder on the antisymmetrized quotient TimelineQuot requires proving that CanonicalR is irreflexive (using the IRR derivation rule). Without this, the Cantor isomorphism cannot be applied. This is the same blocker identified in the Boneyard at RestrictedFragment.lean:434-444.

partial_progress.details: Phase 5 completed with zero sorries: DenseTimeline.lean creates density-enriched staged build with all Cantor prerequisites proven (density intermediates, countability, nonemptiness, no endpoints at CanonicalR level, linearity). Phase 6 blocked: NoMaxOrder on antisymmetrized quotient requires CanonicalR irreflexivity (from IRR rule), which is not yet proven.

completion_data.completion_summary: Completed Phase 5 (Cantor Prerequisites Verification) with zero sorries by creating DenseTimeline.lean, a density-enriched staged build using density_frame_condition intermediates. Proved density, countability, nonemptiness, no-endpoints, and linearity for the dense timeline. Phase 6 blocked on CanonicalR irreflexivity needed for NoMaxOrder on the antisymmetrized quotient.

errors.message: NoMaxOrder on TimelineQuot requires CanonicalR irreflexivity, not yet proven. A reflexive MCS (CanonicalR(M,M)) may be maximal in the quotient ordering.
errors.recommendation: Prove canonicalR_irreflexive using the Gabbay IRR derivation rule (DerivationTree.irr). With irreflexivity, mutual CanonicalR implies equality, all strictness gaps close, and Cantor prerequisites are satisfied.
